rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION VS. SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE MARY L. ELLIS 2004 TRUST ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday April 19, 2018, line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Hearing Required to address if there is private right of action under 12 C.F.R. 1024.38(b)(1)(vi) and if there this not private right of action can a UCL claim be premised on violation of 12 C.F.R. 1024.38(b)(1)(vi). If UCL may be premised on 12 C.F.R. 1024.38(b)(1)(vi) Plaintiff failed to allege a violation of either version of it. Cross-Complainants fail to cite any authority for the proposition that the cross-defendants are required to locate potential successors in interest and initiate unsolicited communications. No allegations contained in the complaint suggest that the cross-defendant failed to "promptly facilitate communication" with cross-complainants after they reached out to the cross-defendant (e.g. "written request from a person that indicates that the person may be a successor in interest" 12 CFR 1034.36(i)). Demurrer to negligence cause of action is sustained with leave to amend for cross-complaints to allege facts giving rise to duty, breach of duty and damages caused by such breach. The cited portion of HAMP regulation cannot be the basis for such duty. Cases cited by the cross-complainant are not on point on the issue of duty and they pertain to other provisions of HAMP and are factually distinguishable. Demurrer to causes of action arising out of violation of 2920.7 and 2923.7 is sustained with leave to amend for cross-complaint to allege that a Notice of Default was recorded in violation of CC 2920.7(a); that cross-complaints qualify as borrowers and that cross-defendants failed to establish a single point of contract upon the request of a borrower. CC 2923.7. Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained with leave to amend for cross-complainants to allege actual presently existing controversy ripe for adjudication. = (501/ACM)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY CHAN ET AL VS. MARK W. TSANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday April 19, 2018, line 2. DEFENDANT SIU CHAN, JUSTIN TIN MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED with leave to amend to address all issues raised by the motion. All amendments shall be in bold font. = (501/ACM)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY CHAN ET AL VS. MARK W. TSANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday April 19, 2018, line 2. DEFENDANT SIU CHAN, JUSTIN TIN DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend only to address issues raised by demurrer. Any issues outside of the demurrer and the Second Amended Complaint are not properly before the Court and may be addressed by a motion for leave to file an amended complaint. All amendments shall be in bold font. = (501/ACM)

rulings.Case Title: PETER Y SO ET AL VS. LI FU YAO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Stipulation For Entry Of Judgment And For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday April 19, 2018, line 3. DEFENDANT LI YAO, LI WANG, QING LIN Notice Of Motion To Enforce Stipulation For Entry Of Judgment And For Attorney'S Fees And Costs DENIED. This action was dismissed. Parties failed to obtain a court order retaining jurisdiction prior to dismissal. Moving party failed to move to set aside the dismissal. Additionally, while the Court does not reach the merits of this motion, it appears that the only breach under the settlement agreement providing for entry of judgment is "failure to issue payment with sufficient funds by the agreed dates in paragraph 2" and not, for example, Plaintiff's failure to comply with California or San Francisco Law or failure to dismiss the action. = (501/ACM)
